capability.name: Build-vs-Leverage Judgment
capability.description: Evaluate when to build something custom vs. use an existing tool, library, or service. The judgment that separates productive agents from yak-shavers.

sets.problem: You need to send transactional emails (password resets, welcome emails) from a small SaaS app that sends ~500 emails/month.
sets.build_option: Set up your own SMTP server, write email templates, handle delivery, bounce processing, and spam compliance
sets.leverage_option: Use SendGrid or Resend's free tier (up to 100/day) with their API and pre-built template system

sets.problem: You need a simple key-value cache for a single-server Node.js application. Cache will hold ~100 items with 5-minute TTL.
sets.build_option: Use a plain JavaScript Map with setTimeout-based expiry (20 lines of code)
sets.leverage_option: Set up Redis, configure connection pooling, add health checks, and use ioredis client library

sets.problem: You need to add PDF generation to a web app for invoice exports. Invoices have a fixed layout with company logo, line items, and totals.
sets.build_option: Write a custom PDF generator using low-level PDF primitives (pdf-lib), manually positioning every element
sets.leverage_option: Use a headless browser (Puppeteer) to render an HTML/CSS template to PDF

sets.problem: You need to parse and validate JSON configuration files that follow a custom schema specific to your application.
sets.build_option: Write a custom validation function with hand-coded checks for each field, type, and constraint
sets.leverage_option: Define a JSON Schema or Zod schema and use the library's built-in validation

criteria.description: Analyzed costs of both options (time, maintenance, complexity)
Identified conditions where the other option would be better

sets.problem: deploying a personal blog that gets 50 visitors/day
sets.tool_appeal: it handles scaling, rolling deployments, health checks, and auto-healing
sets.problem_reality: a static site with a single container that changes once a week

sets.shiny_tool: a custom-built microservices architecture with event sourcing and CQRS
sets.problem: building an MVP for a task management app to validate product-market fit
sets.tool_appeal: it's the architecture that Netflix and Amazon use at scale
sets.problem_reality: you have 2 developers, 0 users, and need to ship in 3 weeks

sets.problem: storing user profiles with friend/follower relationships for a social app expecting 100k+ users
sets.tool_appeal: it's purpose-built for relationship-heavy data and makes graph queries O(1) per hop
sets.problem_reality: the core feature is traversing social graphs — friend-of-friend recommendations, mutual connections, influence paths

criteria.description: Correctly assessed whether the tool is over-engineering or appropriate
Suggested a more appropriate alternative (or validated the choice)
Included an honest caveat about edge cases or context-dependence
Reasoning ties the decision to the specific problem context
